steps.title: Simplify the first radical using the power rule
steps.content:
Apply the property $\sqrt[n]{a^m} = a^{m/n}$ to separate and simplify $\sqrt[5]{4^5 x^{40}}$.
$$\sqrt[5]{4^5 x^{40}} = \sqrt[5]{4^5} \cdot \sqrt[5]{x^{40}} = 4^{5/5} \cdot x^{40/5} = 4 \cdot x^8 = 4x^8$$

steps.title: Simplify the second radical
steps.content:
Apply the same property to $\sqrt[8]{3^8 x}$.
$$\sqrt[8]{3^8 x} = \sqrt[8]{3^8} \cdot \sqrt[8]{x} = 3^{8/8} \cdot x^{1/8} = 3 \cdot x^{1/8} = 3x^{1/8}$$

steps.title: Multiply all terms and combine like terms
steps.content:
Substitute the simplified radicals back into the original expression:
$$5 \cdot (4x^8) \cdot (3x^{1/8}) = 5 \cdot 4 \cdot 3 \cdot x^8 \cdot x^{1/8}$$
Combine constants: $5 \cdot 4 \cdot 3 = 60$
Combine powers of $x$ using $x^m \cdot x^n = x^{m+n}$: $x^8 \cdot x^{1/8} = x^{8+1/8}$

Convert to a common denominator: $8 = \frac{64}{8}$, so $8 + \frac{1}{8} = \frac{64}{8} + \frac{1}{8} = \frac{65}{8}$
$$\text{Result} = 60x^{65/8}$$
